title: Medical Appliance Technicians
description: Construct, maintain, or repair medical supportive devices such as braces, orthotics and prosthetic devices, joints, arch supports, and other surgical and medical appliances.

tasks.text: Drill and tap holes for rivets, and glue, weld, bolt, or rivet parts together to form prosthetic or orthotic devices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Read prescriptions or specifications to determine the type of product or device to be fabricated and the materials and tools required.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Make orthotic or prosthetic devices, using materials such as thermoplastic and thermosetting materials, metal alloys and leather, and hand or power tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Bend, form, and shape fabric or material to conform to prescribed contours of structural components.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Construct or receive casts or impressions of patients' torsos or limbs for use as cutting and fabrication patterns.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Repair, modify, or maintain medical supportive devices, such as artificial limbs, braces, or surgical supports, according to specifications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Cover or pad metal or plastic structures or devices, using coverings such as rubber, leather, felt, plastic, or fiberglass.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Test medical supportive devices for proper alignment, movement, or biomechanical stability, using meters and alignment fixtures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Lay out and mark dimensions of parts, using templates and precision measuring instruments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fit appliances onto patients, and make any necessary adjustments.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Polish artificial limbs, braces, or supports, using grinding and buffing wheels.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Take patients' body or limb measurements for use in device construction.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Instruct patients in use of prosthetic or orthotic devices.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Service or repair machinery used in the fabrication of appliances.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mix pigments to match patients' skin coloring, according to formulas, and apply mixtures to orthotic or prosthetic devices.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
